  3138→%======================================================================
  3139→\section{The Electron Mass and the Lepton Seed}
  3140→\label{sec:electron}
  3141→%======================================================================
  3142→
  3143→The charged lepton triple $(e, \mu, \tau)$ is the ``almost-seed'': 
  3144→$\zch_e = 0.715\MeV^{1/2}$ is small but nonzero. The electron's 
  3145→charge is near zero because of cancellation in $\zch_e = z_0 + z_e$:
  3146→\begin{equation}
  3147→v_0 = 17.716, \quad z_e = -17.001, \quad 
  3148→\zch_e = 17.716 - 17.001 = 0.715\MeV^{1/2}\,.
  3149→\end{equation}
  3150→A 96\% cancellation. The mass $m_e = 0.715^2 = 0.511\MeV$ is the 
  3151→square of something already small.
  3152→
  3153→The near-vanishing of $m_e$ is a structural property of the Koide 
  3154→embedding, not fine-tuning: the energy balance allows perturbations 
  3155→as large as $z_0$. Every Koide triple has one member with 
  3156→$|\zch|/v_0$ small:
  3157→
  3158→\begin{center}
  3159→\begin{tabular}{lccl}
  3160→\toprule
  3161→Triple & Lightest & $|\zch|/v_0$ & Character \\
  3162→\midrule
  3163→$(e, \mu, \tau)$ & $e$ & 0.040 & Almost-seed \\
  3164→$(-\pi, D_s, B)$ & $\pi$ (neg.) & 0.337 & Full triple \\
  3165→$(c, b, t)$ & $c$ & 0.236 & Full triple \\
  3166→$(-s, c, b)$ & $s$ (neg.) & 0.320 & Full triple \\
  3167→\bottomrule
  3168→\end{tabular}
  3169→\end{center}
  3170→
  3171→The electron is by far the most extreme case, consistent with the 
  3172→lepton triple being nearest to its seed.
  3173→
  3174→%======================================================================
  3175→\section{Symmetry Restoration}
  3176→\label{sec:restoration}
  3177→%======================================================================
  3178→
  3179→The broken spectrum reassembles as symmetry-breaking effects are 
  3180→removed:
  3181→
  3182→\begin{enumerate}
  3183→\item \textbf{Turn off QED:} Electromagnetic splittings vanish 
  3184→($\pi^\pm = \pi^0$, $K^\pm = K^0$). D-term effects disappear.
  3185→\item \textbf{Set $m_u = m_d$:} Exact isospin.
  3186→\item \textbf{Set $m_s = m_d$:} $SU(3)_V$ restored, 
  3187→$\pi = K = \eta$. Koide splitting $v_1 \to 0$; lepton partners 
  3188→degenerate.
  3189→\item \textbf{Send $m_q \to 0$:} Chiral limit, all meson charges 
  3190→$\to 0$. If sBootstrap SUSY restores, all light lepton masses 
  3191→vanish too. This is the seed configuration: $(0, 0, 0)$.
  3192→\end{enumerate}
  3193→
  3194→The seed triples are the ``penultimate'' stage: one member has 
  3195→reached zero, but the other two retain the $2 - \sqrt{3}$ ratio. 
  3196→Full triples are the final broken state. The lepton triple, 
  3197→trapped between seed and bloom, reveals the hierarchy: 
  3198→chiral breaking $\to$ seed $\to$ bloom $\to$ full spectrum.
  3199→
  3200→The direction of the flow also matters: approaching the chiral 
  3201→limit, the pion charge rises from $\zch = -11.81$ toward zero 
  3202→from below. It crosses zero exactly in the chiral limit. This 
  3203→means the pion was ``born negative''---its charge sign is set by 
  3204→the direction of chiral symmetry breaking, and the seed 
  3205→$(0, \pi, D_s)$ is the configuration at the moment of crossing.
  3206→
  3207→%======================================================================
  3208→%======================================================================
  3209→\section{Open Problems}
  3210→\label{sec:open}
  3211→%======================================================================
  3212→
  3213→\subsection{The diquark sector}
  3214→The Pauli theorem (\S\ref{subsec:pauli}) proves that no $J=0$,
  3215→color-$\bar{3}$ diquark can have symmetric flavor.  The argument
  3216→extends to all partial waves: $J = 0$ forces $L = S$, so the
  3217→spatial--spin exchange sign is $(-1)^{L+S} = (-1)^{2S} = +1$,
  3218→which combined with the antisymmetric color $\bar{3}$ gives
  3219→overall $+1$; Pauli then requires antisymmetric flavor
  3220→($\overline{\mathbf{10}}$).  The $\mathbf{15}$ is accessible
  3221→only for $J \geq 1$.  In SQCD, the same conclusion holds
  3222→algebraically: the $\varepsilon$-tensor on color indices in
  3223→$B^{abc} = \varepsilon_{ijk}Q^{ia}Q^{jb}Q^{kc}$ forces
  3224→antisymmetric flavor regardless of statistics.
  3225→The sBootstrap's $\mathbf{15}$ diquark must therefore be non-composite:
  3226→either an algebraic image of the Miyazawa supercharge
  3227→(\S\ref{subsec:baryon_open}) or a fundamental $SO(32)$ string state.
  3228→However, the mixed anticommutator
  3229→$\{Q_\alpha, \mathcal{Q}_{[ij],\beta}\} = \epsilon_{\alpha\beta}\,
  3230→\mathcal{S}_{[ij]}$ produces a diquark generator in
  3231→$\overline{\mathbf{10}}$ (antisymmetric), not the symmetric
  3232→$\mathbf{15}$; thus the Miyazawa route alone does not resolve the
  3233→$\mathbf{15}$~vs.~$\overline{\mathbf{10}}$ conflict.
  3234→The exotic $+4/3$-charged
  3235→diquarks of the $\mathbf{15}$ would be a testable consequence:
  3236→if their mass is set by the SUSY-breaking scale ($\sim f_\pi$), they
  3237→would be sub-GeV and accessible at fixed-target experiments; if by
  3238→$\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}$ or higher, LHC pair-production searches for
  3239→exotic charges apply. No mass prediction can be made without a
  3240→concrete realization of the $\mathbf{15}$.
  3241→
  3242→\subsection{What determines the Koide phase?}
  3243→The energy balance constrains but does not uniquely fix $\delta$.
  3244→The O'Raifeartaigh realization (\S\ref{subsec:fterm}) fixes the
  3245→seed ratio $gv/m = \sqrt{3}$ but leaves $\delta$ undetermined---the
  3246→phase controls how the seed blooms into a full triple. The bloom
  3247→is a phase rotation $\delta: 3\pi/4 \to \delta_{\text{phys}}$ at
  3248→fixed $v_0$, but explicit R-breaking perturbations push $Q$ away
  3249→from $2/3$ at any nonzero strength, making the seed an unstable
  3250→fixed point. The bloom must therefore be nonperturbative.
  3251→
  3252→The lepton phase satisfies $\delta_\ell \bmod 2\pi/3 = 2/9$ to
  3253→$33\,\text{ppm}$ ($0.9\sigma$ in $m_\tau$; $3.1\sigma$ after
  3254→look-elsewhere over fractions with denominator $\leq 20$). Since $2/9$
  3255→is not a rational multiple of $\pi$, this is not a geometric angle.
  3256→It is, however, $2/9 = 2/(3\times 3)$, which points to a $\mathbb{Z}_9$
  3257→discrete symmetry in the Koide modulus space. The $\mathbb{Z}_3$
  3258→mass-permutation symmetry ($\delta \to \delta + 2\pi/3$) is the
  3259→unique subgroup; the quotient $\mathbb{Z}_9/\mathbb{Z}_3$ has three
  3260→cosets, producing three inequivalent mass spectra from the same $M_0$.
  3261→The simplest potential with $\mathbb{Z}_9$ symmetry,
  3262→$V \supset -\cos\bigl(9(\delta - 2/9)\bigr)$, has the lepton value
  3263→as one of its nine minima, with the $\mathbb{Z}_3$-equivalent minima
  3264→at $\delta = 2/9 + 2k\pi/3$.
  3265→
  3266→A dynamical origin for $\mathbb{Z}_9$ exists in SQCD: for $N_f = N_c = 3$,
  3267→the diagonal $U(1)_{R+2A}$ (R-symmetry plus twice the axial symmetry)
  3268→has anomaly coefficient $6N_c = 18 = 2 \times 9$. Instantons break
  3269→$U(1)_{R+2A} \to \mathbb{Z}_{18}$, which contains $\mathbb{Z}_9$
  3270→as a subgroup. The meson field $M$ carries $\mathbb{Z}_{18}$ charge 4
  3271→(from two quark zero modes at charge 2 each), so $\det M$ carries
  3272→charge~12.  The lowest-order holomorphic $\mathbb{Z}_{18}$-singlet
  3273→beyond the Seiberg constraint is the three-instanton operator
  3274→\begin{equation}
  3275→  \delta W_{3\text{-inst}}
  3276→    = c_3\,\frac{(\det M)^3}{\Lambda^{18}}\,,
  3277→\end{equation}
  3278→since $(\det M)^3$ has $\mathbb{Z}_{18}$ charge $36 = 2\times 18$.
  3279→Near the SUSY vacuum $\det M = \Lambda^6$, parametrizing
  3280→$\det M = \Lambda^6\,e^{3i\delta_M}$ yields
  3281→$\delta W \propto e^{9i\delta}$, and the cross-term with the
  3282→tree-level superpotential gives
  3283→$V_{\text{eff}} \supset -V_0\cos\bigl(9(\delta - \delta_0)\bigr)$
  3284→with $\delta_0 = \arg(c_3)/9$.
  3285→This is exactly the $\cos(9\delta)$ potential identified above.
  3286→The one-instanton vertex ($\det M$, charge 12) is already captured
  3287→by the Seiberg constraint; the two-instanton vertex ($(\det M)^2$,
  3288→charge 24, not a $\mathbb{Z}_{18}$ singlet) is forbidden.
  3289→The three-instanton correction does not affect the meson mass
  3290→spectrum: the $F$-term equations define an effective Lagrange
  3291→multiplier $X_{\text{eff}} = X + 3c_3\Lambda^{12}/\Lambda^{18}$,
  3292→but the constraint $\det M = \Lambda^6$ and the meson VEVs
  3293→$M_j = C/m_j$ are unmodified. The correction acts only on the
  3294→phase structure (through $\cos(9\delta)$), not on the magnitudes.
  3295→
  3296→At strong coupling ($\Lambda \sim 300\MeV$), the coefficient
  3297→$c_3$ is $O(1)$ and the potential strength is
  3298→$V_0^{1/4} \sim 65\text{--}85\MeV$, comparable to
  3299→$f_\pi = 92\MeV$---this is not a parametrically suppressed effect.
  3300→The same $\cos(9\delta)$ structure also arises from magnetic
  3301→bion contributions on $\mathbb{R}^3 \times S^1$, where the
  3302→$\mathbb{Z}_3$-symmetric combination of the SU(3) affine roots
  3303→maps to $\cos(9\delta)$ in the meson phase variable.
  3304→
  3305→The embedding is specific to $N_c = 3$: the condition
  3306→$N_c^2 \mid 6N_c$ requires $N_c \mid 6$, which holds only for
  3307→$N_c = 1, 2, 3, 6$.
  3308→
  3309→The quark Koide phases, by contrast, do \emph{not} sit at
  3310→$\mathbb{Z}_9$ minima: the $(-s,c,b)$ and $(c,b,t)$ phases
  3311→are displaced by $39\%$ and $22\%$ of the $\mathbb{Z}_9$
  3312→spacing, respectively.  This suggests that the lepton and quark
  3313→phases are selected by different dynamical mechanisms---the
  3314→instanton potential for leptons, and possibly the bloom mechanism
  3315→or ISS dynamics for quarks.
  3316→
  3317→The Coleman--Weinberg potential of the minimal O'Raifeartaigh model
  3318→is flat in $\delta$ at one loop---the pseudo-modulus potential depends
  3319→only on the modulus $|\Phi_0|$, not on the Koide phase.  The
  3320→three-instanton potential above provides the leading
  3321→$\delta$-dependent term that lifts this flat direction.
  3322→The \emph{modulus} direction $|\Phi_0|$ is a separate question:
  3323→the CW potential stabilizes $|\langle\Phi_0\rangle|$ at zero,
  3324→but the Koide seed requires $\langle X\rangle/\mu = \sqrt{3}$
  3325→(\S\ref{subsec:superpotential}).
  3326→A negative quartic K\"ahler correction $c\,|X|^4/\Lambda_K^2$
  3327→with $c = -\Lambda_K^2/(12\mu^2)$ pins the pseudo-modulus at
  3328→the K\"ahler pole $v_{\text{pole}} = \sqrt{3}\,\mu$ exactly
  3329→(an algebraic result, not numerical tuning;
  3330→see \S\ref{subsec:superpotential}).
  3331→This coefficient is \emph{not} radiatively generated: the
  3332→one-loop CW quartic in the O'Raifeartaigh model has $c_4 = -20/3$
  3333→at $gf/m^2 = 1$ (exact), which is orders of magnitude too large;
  3334→the ISS one-loop potential produces a positive quartic ($c_{\text{eff}} = +0.014$),
  3335→the wrong sign entirely. The K\"ahler pole mechanism is therefore a
  3336→tree-level statement about the effective K\"ahler potential,
  3337→not a consequence of one-loop dynamics.  However, magnetic bions
  3338→on $\mathbb{R}^3 \times S^1$ generate a quartic K\"ahler correction
  3339→of the correct sign.
  3340→The bion-induced coefficient
  3341→$c_{\text{bion}} = -3\pi^2\,e^{-4\pi/(N_c^2\alpha_s)}\,M^2/\alpha_s^2$
  3342→(where $M^2 = [\prod_f(m_f/\Lambda)]^2$ is the zero-mode mass factor)
  3343→equals $-1/12$ at $\alpha_s = 0.143$ ($S_{\text{bion}} = 9.76$)
  3344→in the ISS model with heavy magnetic quarks ($M^2 = 1$).
  3345→With physical light-quark masses, zero-mode suppression reduces
  3346→$|c_{\text{bion}}|$ by five orders of magnitude; the ISS magnetic
  3347→dual---where the pseudo-modulus lives---is the appropriate
  3348→setting for the bion calculation.
  3349→The three-instanton operator $(\det M)^3$ shifts the Seiberg
  3350→Lagrange multiplier~$X$ (dimension $[\text{mass}]^{-3}$ in the
  3351→confining phase) but this is a different object from the ISS
  3352→pseudo-modulus (dimension $[\text{mass}]^{+1}$ in the magnetic
  3353→dual).
  3354→
  3355→More broadly, the ISS Coleman--Weinberg potential does \emph{not}
  3356→transmit the Koide condition from the UV quark masses to the IR
  3357→meson spectrum.  In the $N_f = 4$ ISS model, the one-loop CW
  3358→masses for the broken mesons $\chi_a$ are nearly degenerate
  3359→($m_{\text{CW}} \approx 27\MeV$ for all four flavors), because
  3360→the CW mass depends on $m_a$ through the affine map
  3361→$F_a = h\mu^2 - m_a$, and all quark masses satisfy
  3362→$m_a \ll h\mu^2$.  The resulting spectrum gives
  3363→$Q \approx 1/3$ (the degenerate limit), regardless of the UV
  3364→value.  This confirms that flavor-sensitive mass structure must
  3365→arise from non-perturbative effects---the bion K\"ahler
  3366→correction (\S\ref{subsec:fterm}), which directly involves
  3367→$\sqrt{m_k}$, rather than the one-loop effective potential.
  3368→
  3369→\subsection{Connecting the three tuples}
  3370→The unified superpotential (\S\ref{subsec:superpotential}) connects
  3371→the light and heavy sectors through a layered structure:
  3372→the seed Koide fixes $m_c/m_s$ (from $gv/m = \sqrt{3}$),
  3373→the $v_0$-doubling fixes $m_b$ (from the K\"ahler sector),
  3374→and the $(c,b,t)$ Koide serves as an overdetermination test.
  3375→After these constraints, only two to three free parameters remain:
  3376→$m_u$, $m_d$ (connected to the Cabibbo angle), and the overall
  3377→scale $m_s$.  The Koide condition does \emph{not} extend to triples
  3378→containing $m_u$ or $m_d$: $Q(m_u,m_d,m_s) = 0.567$ ($-15\%$)
  3379→and $Q(m_u,m_c,m_t) = 0.849$ ($+27\%$), confirming that the
  3380→lightest quarks are free parameters of the framework. The $v_0$-doubling is non-holomorphic and lives in the
  3381→K\"ahler sector: as shown in \S\ref{subsec:fterm}, the bion
  3382→K\"ahler correction $K_{\text{bion}} \sim |\!\sum s_k\sqrt{\hat m_k}|^2$
  3383→generates an effective potential whose minimum enforces
  3384→$\sqrt{m_b} = 3\sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c}$.
  3385→The Hessian has rank~1, constraining exactly the $v_0$-doubling
  3386→combination while leaving $m_s$ and $m_c$ to the Koide seed.
  3387→The extension from $\mathbb{R}^3 \times S^1$ to
  3388→$\mathbb{R}^4$ relies on \"Unsal's continuity conjecture.
  3389→
  3390→\subsection{Lepton masses are not mesino masses}
  3391→The fermion mass matrix $W_{IJ}$ at the Seiberg vacuum has
  3392→light eigenvalues ($\sim 10^{-3}$--$10^{-2}\MeV$) from the
  3393→seesaw between the meson and Lagrange-multiplier sectors.
  3394→These are far too small and too degenerate
  3395→($Q_{\text{light}} = 0.48$, not $2/3$) to reproduce lepton masses.
  3396→The bion-induced K\"ahler correction
  3397→$\delta K_{ij} \sim \zeta^2 \sqrt{m_i m_j}/\Lambda^2 \sim 10^{-7}$
  3398→is negligible at the Seiberg vacuum, and a systematic scan over
  3399→flavour-dependent K\"ahler parameters finds $Q \in [0.35,\,0.50]$,
  3400→never reaching~$2/3$.
  3401→The obstruction is structural: K\"ahler rescaling
  3402→$K^{-1/2}\,W_{IJ}\,K^{-1/2}$ is a congruence transformation
  3403→that preserves the rank of~$W_{IJ}$ and cannot generate new
  3404→mass eigenvalues.
  3405→A further dimensionless obstruction: the holomorphic mesino
  3406→mass ratio $m_{\psi_{us}}/m_{\psi_{ud}} = M_d/M_s = m_s/m_d = 20.0$
  3407→is preserved by any universal K\"ahler rescaling, but the target
  3408→ratio $m_\mu/m_e = 206.8$ differs by a factor~$10.3$.  Matching
  3409→both masses simultaneously would require flavour-dependent
  3410→anomalous dimensions ($\gamma_{ud} \approx 0.25$ vs.\
  3411→$\gamma_{us} \approx 0.46$), which are plausible for a
  3412→strongly coupled theory but not calculable with existing methods.
  3413→Moreover, the K\"ahler expansion breaks down at the seesaw
  3414→vacuum: $M_u/\Lambda \approx 1362$, $M_d/\Lambda \approx 630$,
  3415→and $M_s/\Lambda \approx 31$, so the canonical K\"ahler
  3416→$K = \operatorname{Tr}(M^\dagger M)$ is a poor approximation
  3417→and large non-perturbative corrections are expected.
  3418→Lepton masses in this framework therefore require a mechanism
  3419→beyond the confined-phase K\"ahler potential.
  3420→A natural candidate is a \emph{separate} confining sector.
  3421→$SU(2)$ SQCD with $N_f = 3$ (the s-confining case $N_f = N_c + 1$)
  3422→produces three antisymmetric mesons $L^{ij} = \epsilon^{ab}\Psi^i_a\Psi^j_b$
  3423→with dynamical superpotential
  3424→$W_{\text{dyn}} = L^{12}L^{13}L^{23}/\Lambda_L^5$ (the Pfaffian).
  3425→An O'Raifeartaigh deformation
  3426→\[
  3427→  W = f X_L + m\,L^{12}L^{23} + g\,X_L(L^{12})^2
  3428→    + \frac{\epsilon}{\Lambda_L^5}\,L^{12}L^{13}L^{23}
  3429→\]
  3430→breaks SUSY at a metastable vacuum ($F_{X_L} = f$) while the
  3431→Pfaffian provides a distant SUSY-restoring vacuum.
  3432→At $t \equiv g\langle X_L\rangle/m = \sqrt{3}$
  3433→(selected by a K\"ahler pole, as in the quark sector),
  3434→the fermion mass matrix has the spectrum
  3435→$(0,\,(2-\sqrt{3})m,\,(2+\sqrt{3})m)$
  3436→with $Q = 2/3$ \emph{exactly}.
  3437→This is the same O'Raifeartaigh--Koide seed that generates the quark
  3438→Koide triples; the mechanism is universal, independent of gauge group.
  3439→The bloom rotation handles the mass hierarchy, and a
  3440→confinement scale $\Lambda_L \sim 300\MeV$ gives the
  3441→observed $M_0 = 313.8\MeV$.
  3442→Below $\Lambda_L$, QED running is nearly flavour-universal
  3443→and preserves $Q = 2/3$ to $O(\alpha^2/\pi^2) \sim 10^{-5}$,
  3444→consistent with the observed $9$~ppm deviation.
  3445→A complete specification of the lepton sector---in particular
  3446→the mediation mechanism coupling $L^{ij}$ to the Higgs doublets
  3447→and the derivation of the overall lepton mass scale---is deferred
  3448→to future work.
  3449→
  3450→\subsection{The baryon sector}
  3451→\label{subsec:baryon_open}
  3452→In Seiberg's SQCD with $N_f = N_c = 3$, baryons are part of the
  3453→low-energy spectrum. Their SUSY partners are ``baryonic fermions''
  3454→with no known candidates among observed particles.
  3455→
  3456→The meson--baryon connection requires a Miyazawa supercharge
  3457→\cite{Miyazawa, CattoGursey}, which cannot lie in the
  3458→fundamental~$\mathbf{5}$: the product
  3459→$\mathbf{5}\otimes(\mathbf{1}\oplus\mathbf{24})$ does not
  3460→contain~$\overline{\mathbf{10}}$, so a single-index generator
  3461→cannot map mesons to baryons.  The minimal choice is
  3462→\begin{equation*}
  3463→\mathcal{Q}_{[pq],\alpha}
  3464→  \;\in\; \overline{\mathbf{10}}\;\text{of}\;SU(5)_f\,,
  3465→  \qquad \Delta B = +1\,,
  3466→\end{equation*}
  3467→with transformation law
  3468→$[\mathcal{Q}_{[pq]}, M^i{}_j]
  3469→  = \delta^i_{[p}\,\tilde\psi_{B,\,q]j}
  3470→  - \tfrac{1}{5}\delta^i_j\,\tilde\psi_{B,\,pq}$,
  3471→where $\tilde\psi_{B,\,lm}
  3472→  = \tfrac{1}{6}\epsilon_{lmijk}\,\psi_B^{ijk}$ is the dual
  3473→baryonino.  The anticommutator decomposes as
  3474→\begin{equation*}
  3475→\overline{\mathbf{10}}\otimes\mathbf{10}
  3476→  = \mathbf{1}\oplus\mathbf{24}\oplus\mathbf{75}\,,
  3477→\end{equation*}
  3478→giving $\{\mathcal{Q},\bar{\mathcal{Q}}\}
  3479→  \supset \sigma^\mu P_\mu + T^A + Z$, where $T^A$ are the
  3480→$SU(5)_f$ generators and $Z$ are central charges encoding the
  3481→meson--baryon mass splitting $m_B^2 - m_M^2 = \langle Z\rangle
  3482→\sim\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}^2$.  This Miyazawa SUSY evades the
  3483→Haag--\L{}opusza\'nski--Sohnius theorem because it is an
  3484→approximate symmetry broken at scale~$\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}$,
  3485→analogous to $SU(6)$ spin--flavor symmetry in the quark model.
  3486→The $\mathbf{75}$ channel on the right-hand side
  3487→signals that the full bosonic subalgebra extends beyond
  3488→$SU(5)_f$; its interpretation remains open.
  3489→The $\overline{\mathbf{10}}$ representation of the Miyazawa
  3490→supercharge is the same $SU(5)_f$ multiplet that appears in the
  3491→$SO(32)$ adjoint decomposition (Table~\ref{tab:496}), suggesting
  3492→that the baryon--meson connection and the string embedding may
  3493→share a common algebraic origin.
  3494→
  3495→\subsection{The strong CP problem}
  3496→The principle ``Nature abhors chirality'' predicts $\bar{\theta} = 0$ 
  3497→but provides no mechanism.
  3498→
  3499→\subsection{The kaon--muon puzzle}

2500→coupling of the confining theory. Standard QCD \emph{explains} 
  2501→$f_\pi$ in terms of $\qqbar$ and $\alpha_s$; the sBootstrap 
  2502→\emph{uses} $f_\pi$ as the fundamental parameter, with QCD 
  2503→providing the dynamical origin but not appearing in the mass 
  2504→formula. The absence of QCD factors in \eqref{eq:pimu} is a 
  2505→hint that the relevant symmetry is not $SU(3)_c$ but the 
  2506→hadronic superalgebra that connects pions to leptons.
  2507→
  2508→The lepton ``seed'' has the worst ratio match ($-9\%$), which 
  2509→is consistent with the observation that the lepton triple is 
  2510→\emph{not} a true seed: $m_e \neq 0$, and the electron was never 
  2511→at the flat direction. The leptons are an ``almost-seed'' whose 
  2512→excellent $Q = 0.66666$ arises not from the seed structure but 
  2513→from the smallness of $m_e$, which makes the two conditions 
  2514→approximately degenerate. For the leptons, condition (1) is 
  2515→approximate rather than exact, and condition (2) is correspondingly 
  2516→relaxed.
  2517→
  2518→\subsection{What the $(c,b,t)$ triple tells us}
  2519→
  2520→The $(c,b,t)$ triple ($Q = 0.6695$, $v_0^2 = 29.6\GeV$) has 
  2521→neither a seed nor a meson shadow. It operates at a scale far 
  2522→above $\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}$, suggesting its origin is different:
  2523→
  2524→\begin{enumerate}
  2525→\item It may be governed by the electroweak Higgs mechanism rather
  2526→than the chiral condensate. In a two-Higgs-doublet model with
  2527→$\tan\beta = 1$ (as predicted by the mixed-triple Koide condition,
  2528→\S\ref{subsec:superpotential}), $y_t \approx \sqrt{2}$;
  2529→the Koide condition on $(c,b,t)$ would then constrain the Yukawa
  2530→coupling matrix.
  2531→\item It may represent a Volkov--Akulov realization at the 
  2532→electroweak scale: if there is a second SUSY breaking at 
  2533→$f' \sim v_{\text{EW}}$, the $(c,b,t)$ triple would be its 
  2534→analog of the $(\pi, \mu)$ system, but with $f'$ replacing $f_\pi$.
  2535→\item Or it may be a purely kinematic accident---the quark masses 
  2536→happen to satisfy the Koide condition without a dynamical reason. 
  2537→The 0.42\% precision makes this unlikely but not impossible.
  2538→\end{enumerate}
  2539→
  2540→Interpretation (1) is most natural within the sBootstrap: the
  2541→$(c,b,t)$ triple involves quarks whose masses are dominated by
  2542→Yukawa couplings to the Higgs, not by the chiral condensate.
  2543→Since $m_i = y_i\,v_{\text{EW}}/\sqrt{2}$, the Koide condition
  2544→$Q = 2/3$ translates into a constraint on the Yukawa couplings
  2545→(see \S\ref{subsec:superpotential}):
  2546→$\sum y_i/(\sum \sqrt{y_i})^2 = 2/3$.
  2547→With $y_t \approx 1$ fixed by the top mass, this determines
  2548→$y_b/y_t$ and $y_c/y_t$ through the Koide parametrization.
  2549→The top Yukawa $y_t = 0.99$ is the only coupling of order unity;
  2550→the charm and bottom Yukawas ($y_c = 0.010$, $y_b = 0.034$) are
  2551→effective couplings generated by the Seiberg seesaw, not independent
  2552→fundamental parameters.  In this picture, the entire fermion mass
  2553→hierarchy derives from a single Yukawa coupling ($y_t \approx 1$)
  2554→plus confining dynamics at $\Lambda \sim 300\MeV$.
  2555→The chiral sector and the electroweak sector would each
  2556→independently produce a Koide-type energy balance, with the charm
  2557→and bottom quarks serving as the bridge between the two.
  2558→
  2559→\paragraph{CKM mixing and the Koide triples.}
  2560→A striking feature of the quark Koide triples is that they
  2561→\emph{obligatorily mix up-type and down-type quarks}: $(-s,c,b)$
  2562→contains one up-type ($c$) and two down-type ($s,b$), while
  2563→$(c,b,t)$ contains two up-type ($c,t$) and one down-type ($b$). An
  2564→exhaustive scan of all $\binom{6}{3} = 20$ quark triples with all
  2565→sign choices confirms that \emph{every} triple close to $Q = 2/3$ is
  2566→mixed---neither the pure down-type $(d,s,b)$ nor the pure up-type
  2567→$(u,c,t)$ comes close ($Q = 0.73$ and $Q = 0.85$ respectively).
  2568→This mixing of mass eigenvalues from different Yukawa sectors is
  2569→puzzling in the Standard Model, where up-type and down-type masses are
  2570→eigenvalues of independent matrices $Y_u$ and $Y_d$. It is natural,
  2571→however, in an $SU(5)_f$ flavor-symmetric framework where all five
  2572→quarks are treated democratically.
  2573→
  2574→The Koide constraints on the two overlapping triples, together with
  2575→the seed condition on $(0,s,c)$, determine $(s,c,b)$ from a single
  2576→input ($m_t$). The unconstrained masses are $m_u$ and $m_d$---the
  2577→first generation. The Gatto--Sartori--Tonin relation~\cite{GST1968} $|V_{us}| \approx
  2578→\sqrt{m_d/m_s}$ connects precisely these free parameters to the
  2579→Cabibbo angle ($|V_{us}| = 0.224$ vs.\ PDG $0.2243$,
  2580→Table~\ref{tab:predictions}), suggesting that the CKM matrix
  2581→encodes the residual freedom left undetermined by the Koide
  2582→structure.
  2583→
  2584→If interpretation (2) is also invoked, the sBootstrap has two breaking 
  2585→scales:
  2586→\begin{equation}
  2587→f_{\text{chiral}} = f_\pi \approx 92\MeV\,, \qquad
  2588→f_{\text{EW}} \sim v_{\text{EW}} \approx 246\GeV\,,
  2589→\end{equation}
  2590→and the full spectrum requires both. The light sector (leptons, 
  2591→light mesons) is governed by $f_\pi$; the heavy sector (top, and 
  2592→by extension charm and bottom through the Koide network) is 
  2593→governed by $v_{\text{EW}}$. The charm and bottom quarks, 
  2594→appearing in both the $(0,s,c)/(-s,c,b)$ light-sector triples 
  2595→and the $(c,b,t)$ heavy-sector triple, are the ``bridges'' 
  2596→between the two scales.
  2597→
  2598→\subsection{Toward a Superpotential}
  2599→\label{subsec:superpotential}
  2600→
  2601→Consider $\mathcal{N}=1$ SQCD with gauge group $SU(3)_c$ and $N_f=5$ flavors
  2602→of chiral superfields $Q^i$, $\bar Q_j$ ($i,j=1\ldots 5$, corresponding to
  2603→$u,d,s,c,b$) in the fundamental and antifundamental representations. The
  2604→tree-level superpotential is
  2605→%
  2606→\begin{equation*}
  2607→  W_{\text{tree}} = \operatorname{Tr}(\hat m\, M)\,,
  2608→  \qquad
  2609→  \hat m = \operatorname{diag}(m_u,\, m_d,\, m_s,\, m_c,\, m_b)\,,
  2610→\end{equation*}
  2611→%
  2612→where $M^i{}_j = Q^i\cdot\bar Q_j$ is the $5\times 5$ composite meson
  2613→superfield. Since $m_c, m_b \gg \Lambda_{\text{QCD}}$, the charm and
  2614→bottom quarks are integrated out at tree level, reducing the effective
  2615→theory to $N_f = N_c = 3$ light flavors $(u,d,s)$. In this regime,
  2616→Seiberg's exact results give confinement with the composite meson
  2617→$M^{(3)}$, baryons $B$, $\bar B$, and the quantum-modified
  2618→constraint~\eqref{eq:seiberg}:
  2619→%
  2620→\begin{equation*}
  2621→  \det M^{(3)} - B\bar B = \Lambda_{\text{eff}}^{6}\,,
  2622→\end{equation*}
  2623→%
  2624→where $\Lambda_{\text{eff}}$ is the dynamical scale of the effective
  2625→three-flavor theory after threshold matching at $m_c$ and $m_b$.
  2626→Implementing this constraint via a Lagrange multiplier $X$ gives the full
  2627→effective superpotential
  2628→%
  2629→\begin{equation*}
  2630→  W_{\text{eff}}
  2631→  = \operatorname{Tr}(\hat m^{(3)}\, M^{(3)})
  2632→  + X\!\left(\det M^{(3)} - B\bar B - \Lambda_{\text{eff}}^{6}\right).
  2633→\end{equation*}
  2634→
  2635→The $F$-term equations for the $3\times 3$ light-flavor block read
  2636→%
  2637→\begin{align*}
  2638→  \frac{\partial W_{\text{eff}}}{\partial M^i{}_j}
  2639→  &= \hat m_j\,\delta^i{}_j
  2640→    + X\,(\det M^{(3)})\,(M^{(3)\,-1})_i{}^j = 0\,,\\[4pt]
  2641→  \frac{\partial W_{\text{eff}}}{\partial B}
  2642→  &= -X\,\bar B = 0\,,\\[4pt]
  2643→  \frac{\partial W_{\text{eff}}}{\partial \bar B}
  2644→  &= -X\, B = 0\,.
  2645→\end{align*}
  2646→%
  2647→The baryon equations force either $X=0$ or $B=\bar B=0$. If $X=0$ the meson
  2648→equation reduces to $\hat m_j\,\delta^i{}_j=0$, which has no solution for
  2649→nonzero quark masses. Hence $B=\bar B=0$ and the constraint becomes
  2650→$\det M^{(3)} = \Lambda_{\text{eff}}^6$. The diagonal meson equation then requires
  2651→%
  2652→\begin{equation*}
  2653→  \hat m_j + X\,\frac{\det M^{(3)}}{M^j{}_j} = 0
  2654→  \qquad(\text{no sum on } j)\,,
  2655→\end{equation*}
  2656→%
  2657→which determines each diagonal entry of $M^{(3)}$ in terms of the quark masses and
  2658→$\Lambda_{\text{eff}}$. For a seed configuration in which one quark mass vanishes---say
  2659→$m_u\to 0$---the corresponding $F$-term
  2660→$F_{M^1{}_1}=X\,(\det M^{(3)})(M^{(3)\,-1})_1{}^1$ remains nonzero so long as
  2661→$\det M^{(3)}\neq 0$, which is guaranteed by the quantum constraint. Supersymmetry
  2662→is therefore spontaneously broken, with order parameter
  2663→%
  2664→\begin{equation*}
  2665→  \langle F \rangle \;\sim\; \fpi^2\,.
  2666→\end{equation*}
  2667→
  2668→The scalar and fermionic components of $M^i{}_j$ acquire different masses
  2669→through this breaking. The scalar component (pseudoscalar meson $q_i\bar q_j$)
  2670→picks up an $F$-term contribution absent for the fermionic component (lepton,
  2671→via the Miyazawa superalgebra):
  2672→%
  2673→\begin{align*}
  2674→  m^2_{M^i{}_j}\big|_{\text{scalar}}
  2675→  &= |m_i + m_j|^2 + f^2\,,\\[3pt]
  2676→  m^2_{M^i{}_j}\big|_{\text{fermion}}
  2677→  &= |m_i + m_j|^2\,,
  2678→\end{align*}
  2679→%
  2680→where $f^2\approx \fpi^2$. The individual masses $|m_i + m_j|$ are
  2681→the tree-level (perturbative) contributions; the physical masses are
  2682→dominated by nonperturbative QCD effects that shift both meson and
  2683→lepton by the same amount.  The mass \emph{splitting}, however,
  2684→is universal across the multiplet:
  2685→%
  2686→\begin{equation*}
  2687→  m^2_{\text{meson}} - m^2_{\text{lepton}} = f^2 \approx \fpi^2\,,
  2688→\end{equation*}
  2689→%
  2690→because $f^2$ is a SUSY-breaking contribution independent of the QCD
  2691→dynamics.  This is precisely the Volkov--Akulov
  2692→relation~\eqref{eq:pimu}.
  2693→For heavier mesons the actual splitting is strongly flavor-dependent
  2694→(\S\ref{sec:open}, ``kaon--muon puzzle'').
  2695→
  2696→The top quark, with $m_t\gg\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}$, does not hadronize and
  2697→therefore does not participate in the confined meson matrix---the counting
  2698→$N_f=5$ is exact. Its mass, and corrections to the heavy-quark sector
  2699→($c$, $b$), originate instead from electroweak symmetry breaking.
  2700→In $\mathcal{N}=1$ SUSY, holomorphy of the superpotential requires
  2701→separate Higgs doublets $H_u$ and $H_d$ for up-type and down-type
  2702→quarks, giving
  2703→%
  2704→\begin{equation*}
  2705→  W_{\text{Yukawa}}
  2706→  = y^u_{ij}\, H_u\, Q^i\, \bar Q_j
  2707→  + y^d_{ij}\, H_d\, Q^i\, \bar Q_j\,,
  2708→\end{equation*}
  2709→%
  2710→with $\langle H_u\rangle = v\sin\beta/\sqrt{2}$ and
  2711→$\langle H_d\rangle = v\cos\beta/\sqrt{2}$
  2712→($v = 246\GeV$, $\tan\beta = v_u/v_d$).
  2713→This is relevant only for quarks whose masses are dominated by the
  2714→electroweak condensate rather than the chiral condensate. For the light sector
  2715→($u,d,s$) the Yukawa contribution is negligible compared to
  2716→$\operatorname{Tr}(\hat m\, M)$. Note that $\hat m$ in the chiral
  2717→mass term already \emph{is} the current quark mass generated by the
  2718→Higgs mechanism; the two terms are not additive.  The Yukawa
  2719→superpotential is written separately to make explicit the dependence
  2720→on the Higgs doublets, which are needed for the heavy sector where
  2721→$m_i \gg \Lambda_{\text{QCD}}$ and the mass cannot be attributed to
  2722→chiral symmetry breaking.
  2723→
  2724→The two-doublet structure has a direct consequence for the Koide
  2725→triples. Since the quark Koide triples obligatorily mix up-type
  2726→and down-type quarks (\S\ref{subsec:cbt}), the translation from
  2727→masses to Yukawa couplings depends on~$\tan\beta$.
  2728→For the $(c,b,t)$ triple, with $c$ and $t$ receiving mass
  2729→from $H_u$ and $b$ from~$H_d$:
  2730→%
  2731→\begin{equation*}
  2732→  Q(m_c, m_b, m_t)
  2733→  = Q(y_c\sin\beta,\; y_b\cos\beta,\; y_t\sin\beta)\,,
  2734→\end{equation*}
  2735→%
  2736→which equals $Q(y_c, y_b, y_t)$ only when $\sin\beta = \cos\beta$,
  2737→i.e., $\tan\beta = 1$. The same conclusion holds for the $(-s,c,b)$
  2738→triple, where $s$ and $b$ come from $H_d$ and $c$ from $H_u$.
  2739→Thus $\tan\beta = 1$ is the unique value at which the Koide condition
  2740→on \emph{masses} is equivalent to a Koide condition on
  2741→\emph{Yukawa couplings} for mixed up/down-type triples.
  2742→
  2743→This prediction is independently motivated by the sBootstrap's
  2744→$SU(5)_f$ flavor symmetry, which treats all five quarks
  2745→democratically.  A ratio $\tan\beta \neq 1$ would distinguish
  2746→up-type from down-type quarks in the vacuum, breaking $SU(5)_f$
  2747→at the electroweak scale in a way not present in the chiral sector.
  2748→Equal Higgs VEVs, $v_u = v_d = v/\sqrt{2} = 174\GeV$,
  2749→preserve the democratic structure.
  2750→At $\tan\beta = 1$, the top Yukawa is
  2751→$y_t = \sqrt{2}\,m_t/v_u = 2m_t/v \approx 1.40 \approx \sqrt{2}$.
  2752→
  2753→The Koide condition on the $(c,b,t)$ triple constrains the diagonal
  2754→Yukawa matrix $Y = \operatorname{diag}(y_c,y_b,y_t)$.
  2755→At $\tan\beta = 1$, all Yukawas are
  2756→$y_i = 2m_i/v$ regardless of type, and the Koide relation reads
  2757→%
  2758→\begin{equation*}
  2759→  \frac{y_c + y_b + y_t}
  2760→       {(\sqrt{y_c}+\sqrt{y_b}+\sqrt{y_t})^2}
  2761→  = \frac{2}{3}\,,
  2762→\end{equation*}
  2763→%
  2764→which is equivalent to writing the square roots of the Yukawa
  2765→couplings as
  2766→%
  2767→\begin{equation*}
  2768→  \sqrt{y_k} = \sqrt{y_0}\,
  2769→  \bigl(1 + \sqrt{2}\,\cos(2\pi k/3 + \delta)\bigr)\,,
  2770→  \qquad k = 0,1,2\,,
  2771→\end{equation*}
  2772→%
  2773→where $y_0 = \bigl(\sum\sqrt{y_k}\bigr)^2\!/9$ and $\delta$ is
  2774→the Koide phase. For the physical $(c,b,t)$ masses,
  2775→$\delta \approx 0.069$ and $Q = 0.6695$ (a 0.42\% deviation from
  2776→$2/3$). The traceless part of $\sqrt{Y}$ lies in the Cartan
  2777→subalgebra of $SU(3)_f$:
  2778→%
  2779→\begin{equation*}
  2780→  \sqrt{Y} = \sqrt{y_0}\,
  2781→  \Bigl(I_3 + \tfrac{\sqrt{6}}{2}\,
  2782→  \hat n\cdot\boldsymbol\lambda_{\text{diag}}\Bigr)\,,
  2783→\end{equation*}
  2784→%
  2785→where $\boldsymbol\lambda_{\text{diag}} = (\lambda_3, \lambda_8)$
  2786→are the diagonal Gell-Mann matrices and
  2787→$\hat n = (\cos\theta,\,\sin\theta)$ is a unit vector
  2788→in the Cartan plane.  An exact trigonometric identity gives
  2789→$\theta = \pi/6 - \delta$, so the Koide phase is literally
  2790→the angle of the Yukawa matrix in flavor space.
  2791→The Koide condition fixes the amplitude at $\sqrt{6}/2$; only
  2792→$\hat n$ (equivalently $\delta$) is free.
  2793→The bloom from seed to physical triple is a rigid rotation in
  2794→the $(\lambda_3,\lambda_8)$ plane by $-\Delta\delta$.
  2795→
  2796→The total Lagrangian is
  2797→%
  2798→\begin{equation*}
  2799→  \mathcal{L}
  2800→  = \int\!d^4\theta\,K
  2801→  + \left[\int\!d^2\theta\,W + \text{h.c.}\right]
  2802→  + \mathcal{L}_D
  2803→  + \mathcal{L}_{\text{soft}}\,,
  2804→\end{equation*}
  2805→%
  2806→with K\"ahler potential (canonical plus stabilization and bion corrections),
  2807→%
  2808→\begin{equation*}
  2809→  K = \underbrace{\operatorname{Tr}(M^\dagger M) + |X|^2 + |B|^2
  2810→    + |\bar B|^2 + |H_u|^2 + |H_d|^2 + |S|^2}_{K_{\text{can}}}
  2811→  \underbrace{{}-\,\frac{|X|^4}{12\,\mu^2}}_{K_{\text{stab}}}
  2812→  + \underbrace{\frac{\zeta^2}{\Lambda^2}\,
  2813→    \Bigl|\sum_{k=1}^{N_c} s_k \sqrt{\hat m_k}\,\Bigr|^2}_{K_{\text{bion}}}\,,
  2814→\end{equation*}
  2815→The stabilization term $K_{\text{stab}} = -|X|^4/(12\mu^2)$ is the
  2816→negative quartic K\"ahler correction from \S\ref{subsec:superpotential}
  2817→(with $\Lambda_K = \mu$, $c = -1/12$); it pins the pseudo-modulus at
  2818→$\langle X\rangle = \sqrt{3}\,\mu$ via the K\"ahler pole.
  2819→The monopole fugacity is $\zeta \propto e^{-S_0/(2N_c)}$,
  2820→$\hat m_k$ are the current quark masses (the couplings in
  2821→$W \ni \operatorname{Tr}(\hat m\,M)$), and
  2822→$s_k = \pm 1$ are monopole-instanton signs (encoding the seed-to-bloom
  2823→phase). The bion term is non-holomorphic (containing $\sqrt{\hat m}$) and
  2824→non-perturbatively suppressed by $e^{-2S_0/3} \sim 10^{-2}$ at the
  2825→confinement scale. Its minimum enforces the $v_0$-doubling condition
  2826→$\sqrt{m_b} = 3\sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c}$
  2827→(\S\ref{subsec:fterm});
  2828→%
  2829→superpotential combining mass, constraint, and Yukawa terms,
  2830→%
  2831→\begin{equation*}
  2832→  W = \underbrace{\operatorname{Tr}(\hat m\, M)}_{\text{chiral mass}}
  2833→    + \underbrace{X\!\left(\det M^{(3)} - B\bar B
  2834→      - \Lambda_{\text{eff}}^{6}\right)}_{\text{Seiberg constraint}}
  2835→    + \underbrace{c_3\,\frac{(\det M^{(3)})^3}
  2836→      {\Lambda_{\text{eff}}^{18}}}_{\text{3-instanton}}
  2837→    + \underbrace{y_c\, H_u\, M^c{}_c
  2838→      + y_b\, H_d\, M^b{}_b
  2839→      + y_t\, H_u\, Q^t \bar Q_t\phantom{X}}_{\text{Yukawa}}
  2840→    + \underbrace{\lambda_S\, S\, H_u\!\cdot\!H_d
  2841→      + \tfrac{\kappa}{3}\,S^3}_{\text{NMSSM}}\,,
  2842→\end{equation*}
  2843→%
  2844→where $M^{(3)}$ is the $3\times 3$ light-flavor block ($u,d,s$)
  2845→and $\Lambda_{\text{eff}}$ is the dynamical scale of the confined
  2846→$N_f = N_c = 3$ theory (including threshold matching at $m_c$
  2847→and $m_b$).  The Yukawa terms are written in the full five-flavor
  2848→theory above the heavy-quark thresholds, where $M^c{}_c = Q^c\bar Q_c$
  2849→and $M^b{}_b = Q^b\bar Q_b$ are elementary bilinears;
  2850→below threshold, they reduce to the matching conditions that generate
  2851→$\Lambda_{\text{eff}}$ and the Seiberg constraint.
  2852→The top Yukawa $y_t\,H_u\,Q^t\bar Q_t$ stands apart because the
  2853→top does not confine into a meson.
  2854→At $\tan\beta = 1$, the tree-level lightest Higgs mass vanishes
  2855→($m_h = m_Z|\cos 2\beta| = 0$); the NMSSM coupling
  2856→$\lambda_S\,S\,H_u\!\cdot\!H_d$ lifts the flat direction and gives
  2857→$m_h = \lambda_S v/\sqrt{2} = 125\GeV$ for $\lambda_S = 0.72$.
  2858→The singlet~$S$ is a \emph{separate} elementary field, distinct
  2859→from the Seiberg Lagrange multiplier~$X$
  2860→(\S\ref{subsec:higgs_open}).
  2861→The constraint $\tan\beta = 1$ ($v_u = v_d = v/\sqrt{2}$) is imposed
  2862→by the Koide condition on mixed triples
  2863→(\S\ref{subsec:superpotential}).
  2864→The $D$-term Lagrangian includes an optional Fayet--Iliopoulos
  2865→parameter $\xi$ for $U(1)_{\text{em}}$:
  2866→%
  2867→\begin{equation*}
  2868→  \mathcal{L}_D
  2869→  = -\frac{e^2}{2}\!\left(\sum_i Q_i\,|\phi_i|^2
  2870→    + \xi\right)^{\!2}.
  2871→\end{equation*}
  2872→%
  2873→Finally, soft SUSY-breaking terms arise from a spurion
  2874→$\langle F_S\rangle = \fpi^2$:
  2875→%
  2876→\begin{equation*}
  2877→  \mathcal{L}_{\text{soft}}
  2878→  = -\tilde m^2 \operatorname{Tr}(M^\dagger M)
  2879→  - \bigl(B_\mu\operatorname{Tr}(\hat m\, M)
  2880→    + A_y\bigl(y_c\, H_u\, M^c{}_c + y_b\, H_d\, M^b{}_b\bigr) + \text{h.c.}\bigr)\,,
  2881→  \qquad
  2882→  \tilde m^2 \sim \fpi^2\,.
  2883→\end{equation*}
  2884→%
  2885→The soft scalar mass $\tilde m^2 \sim \fpi^2 \approx (92\MeV)^2$
  2886→generates the universal meson--lepton splitting
  2887→$m^2_{\text{meson}} - m^2_{\text{lepton}} = \fpi^2$.
  2888→
  2889→\paragraph{Scope of the Lagrangian.}
  2890→This Lagrangian produces: (i)~the seed Koide condition ($gv/m = \sqrt{3}$
  2891→in the O'Raifeartaigh limit); (ii)~the meson--lepton mass splitting
  2892→($\fpi^2$); (iii)~the $v_0$-doubling condition via the bion K\"ahler
  2893→correction $K_{\text{bion}}$, whose minimum enforces
  2894→$\sqrt{m_b} = 3\sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c}$; (iv)~the Yukawa couplings for
  2895→the heavy sector; (v)~the $\cos(9\delta)$ potential that selects
  2896→the lepton Koide phase, via the three-instanton operator
  2897→$(\det M^{(3)})^3/\Lambda_{\text{eff}}^{18}$. The bloom is
  2898→non-holomorphic and lives in the K\"ahler sector: the bion term
  2899→$|\sum s_k\sqrt{\hat m_k}|^2$ is the leading monopole-instanton
  2900→contribution on $\mathbb{R}^3 \times S^1$
  2901→(\S\ref{subsec:fterm}).
  2902→
  2903→\paragraph{Flavour-universal Yukawa identity.}
  2904→At the seesaw vacuum $M_j = C/m_j$, the product of the Yukawa
  2905→coupling times the meson VEV is flavour-independent:
  2906→\[
  2907→  y_j\,\langle M_j\rangle
  2908→  = \frac{m_j}{v_{\text{type}}}\,\frac{C}{m_j}
  2909→  = \frac{C}{v_{\text{type}}}
  2910→  = \frac{\sqrt{2}\,C}{v}
  2911→  = 5.07\;\MeV\,,
  2912→\]
  2913→where $C = \Lambda^2(m_u m_d m_s)^{1/3}$.
  2914→The quark mass cancels exactly: heavy quarks couple weakly
  2915→(small~$y_j$) but have large meson VEVs (large~$M_j$), and
  2916→vice versa.
  2917→This identity ensures that the Higgs $F$-terms from the meson
  2918→sector are flavour-blind, automatically satisfying the
  2919→Glashow--Weinberg condition for the absence of tree-level
  2920→Higgs-mediated FCNCs.
  2921→
  2922→\paragraph{Three Yukawa sectors.}
  2923→The six quarks divide into three sectors with distinct coupling
  2924→structures:
  2925→\begin{center}
  2926→\small
  2927→\begin{tabular}{llll}
  2928→\toprule
  2929→Sector & Quarks & Mechanism & $y$ range \\
  2930→\midrule
  2931→Confined & $u,d,s$ & $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat m\,M)$: mass
  2932→  term is Yukawa & $10^{-5}$--$5\times 10^{-4}$ \\
  2933→Intermediate & $c,b$ & Explicit $y_j H M^j{}_j$ & $7\times 10^{-3}$--$2.4\times 10^{-2}$ \\
  2934→Elementary & $t$ & $y_t H_u Q^t\bar Q_t$ & $0.99 \approx 1$ \\
  2935→\bottomrule
  2936→\end{tabular}
  2937→\end{center}
  2938→At $\tan\beta = 1$, both Yukawa matrices
  2939→$Y^u = \operatorname{diag}(y_u,y_c,y_t)$ and
  2940→$Y^d = \operatorname{diag}(y_d,y_s,y_b)$ are diagonal at
  2941→tree level; CKM mixing must originate in the UV quark mass
  2942→texture rather than in confined-phase meson dynamics
  2943→(\S\ref{subsec:ckm_open}).
  2944→
  2945→\paragraph{Particle spectrum and supertrace.}
  2946→The Lagrangian defines a $16\times 16$ fermion mass matrix and a
  2947→$32\times 32$ scalar mass-squared matrix at the Seiberg vacuum.
  2948→The fermion spectrum spans twenty orders of magnitude, from the
  2949→ultra-heavy $X$--$M^s{}_s$ pair at $\sim\Lambda^6/(M_u M_d)
  2950→\sim 8\times 10^{10}\MeV$ down to baryonic fermions at
  2951→$\sim|X_0| \sim 10^{-9}\MeV$.  The off-diagonal meson fermion
  2952→masses follow the inverted seesaw hierarchy $|X_0|\langle M_k\rangle
  2953→\propto 1/m_k$, transmitting the quark mass ratios to the
  2954→confined spectrum.  The supertrace is
  2955→$\text{STr}[M^2] = 2\operatorname{Tr}(M^2_{\text{soft}})
  2956→= 18\,\fpi^2 = 152{,}352\MeV^2$:
  2957→the $W_{IJ}^2$ piece cancels between scalar and fermion sectors,
  2958→and the holomorphic third-derivative piece cancels between real
  2959→and imaginary scalar components, leaving only the nine
  2960→soft meson masses.
  2961→Decomposed by electromagnetic charge, the supertrace
  2962→becomes $\text{STr}_Q[M^2] = 2\,n_Q\,\fpi^2$ where $n_Q$
  2963→counts meson fields with charge~$Q$: $n_0 = 5$, $n_{\pm 1} = 2$.
  2964→The EM-weighted sum $\sum Q_{\text{em}}^2\,\text{STr}_Q = 8\,\fpi^2$
  2965→is nonzero because only mesons carry soft mass; this is the
  2966→electromagnetic trace that connects to the FI
  2967→parameter~(\S\ref{subsec:dterm}).
  2968→The Yukawa-induced $F$-terms ($F_{M^d{}_d} = y_c v/\sqrt{2}$,
  2969→$F_{M^s{}_s} = y_b v/\sqrt{2}$) are nonzero at the seesaw
  2970→vacuum but do not couple to the off-diagonal meson directions:
  2971→$\partial F_{M^d{}_d}/\partial M^u{}_s\big|_{\text{diag}} = 0$
  2972→because the off-diagonal cofactors vanish at a diagonal matrix.
  2973→All six off-diagonal meson masses are positive
  2974→($m^2 \approx 2\fpi^2 = 16{,}928\;\MeV^2$),
  2975→and one-loop Coleman--Weinberg corrections are stabilising
  2976→($\delta m^2_{\text{CW}} \sim 10^{-9}\,m^2_{\text{tree}}$).
  2977→
  2978→\paragraph{Mass spectrum from the Lagrangian.}
  2979→The mass of each particle is determined by a specific term:
  2980→\begin{center}
  2981→\small
  2982→\begin{tabular}{lll}
  2983→\toprule
  2984→Mass & Origin & Equation \\
  2985→\midrule
  2986→$m_{\text{meson}}^2 - m_{\text{lepton}}^2 = \fpi^2$
  2987→  & $\mathcal{L}_{\text{soft}}$: $\tilde m^2 \sim \fpi^2$ & Universal splitting \\
  2988→$m_c/m_s = (2+\sqrt{3})^2$
  2989→  & $W$: O'Raifeartaigh at $gv/m = \sqrt{3}$ & Seed Koide \\
  2990→$\sqrt{m_b} = 3\sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c}$
  2991→  & $K_{\text{bion}}$: minimum of $|S_{\text{bloom}} - 2S_{\text{seed}}|^2$
  2992→  & $v_0$-doubling \\
  2993→$m_t = y_t v_u/\sqrt{2}$, $\tan\beta = 1$
  2994→  & $W_{\text{Yukawa}}$: $y_t H_u Q^t\bar Q_t$ & External \\
  2995→$\delta m^2 = Q_{\text{em}}\,\xi$
  2996→  & $\mathcal{L}_D$: FI term & EM splitting \\
  2997→$\delta_\ell \bmod 2\pi/3 = 2/9$
  2998→  & $W$: $(\det M^{(3)})^3/\Lambda^{18}$ & 3-instanton \\
  2999→\bottomrule

3500→The Lagrangian predicts a universal soft splitting
  3501→$m^2_{\text{meson}} - m^2_{\text{lepton}} = \fpi^2$ across the
  3502→meson matrix. For the pion--muon pair, this works:
  3503→$m_\pi^2 - m_\mu^2 \approx \fpi^2$ to $2.2\%$. But the
  3504→tree-level formula $m^2_M = |m_i + m_j|^2 + \fpi^2$ badly
  3505→underpredicts heavier meson masses: it gives $m_K \approx 133\MeV$
  3506→(physical: $498\MeV$) and $m_{D_s} \approx 1{,}367\MeV$
  3507→(physical: $1{,}968\MeV$). QCD confinement generates the bulk
  3508→of these masses independently of the soft term. The universal
  3509→splitting applies to the \emph{soft} contribution, not to the
  3510→total mass; it is visible only for $(\pi, \mu)$,
  3511→where the soft term dominates over $|m_u + m_d|^2$.
  3512→
  3513→\subsection{Flavour-changing neutral currents}
  3514→The off-diagonal meson scalars $M^d{}_s$, $M^s{}_d$ are the
  3515→\emph{same particles} as the SM kaon and antikaon---they are not
  3516→new degrees of freedom.  Their tree-level contribution to
  3517→$K$--$\bar K$ mixing is the standard QCD effect already
  3518→included in SM predictions.  The question is whether the
  3519→additional SUSY couplings (the $\lambda_S\,S\,H_u\!\cdot\!H_d$
  3520→vertex, the Yukawa $y_c\,H_u\,M^d{}_d$ and
  3521→$y_b\,H_d\,M^s{}_s$) generate \emph{extra} flavour-violating
  3522→contributions beyond QCD.  Because the mesinos are identified
  3523→with the leptons in the sBootstrap framework, no new particles
  3524→are introduced; the principal source of additional FCNC is the
  3525→Yukawa-meson cross-coupling through Higgs exchange.  A
  3526→quantitative estimate of these effects requires matching the
  3527→effective theory to the measured $K$--$\bar K$ Wilson
  3528→coefficients~\cite{UTfit} and will be addressed in future work.
  3529→
  3530→\subsection{The Casimir eigenvalue equation}
  3531→The eigenvalue equation $x^2 + C_2 x - C_2 = 0$ produces the
  3532→on-shell weak mixing angle to $0.4\sigma$, but three issues
  3533→remain open: (i)~the equation is an ansatz, not derived from a
  3534→Lagrangian; (ii)~the match uses the on-shell renormalization
  3535→scheme, and no argument selects this scheme over $\overline{\text{MS}}$;
  3536→(iii)~the negative spin-$\tfrac{1}{2}$ root gives $|M| = 122.4\GeV$,
  3537→tantalizingly close to $M_H = 125.25\GeV$ but with a spin mismatch.
  3538→
  3539→\subsection{The Higgs mass at $\tan\beta = 1$}
  3540→At $\tan\beta = 1$, the $D$-term quartic
  3541→$(g^2+g'^2)(|H_u|^2 - |H_d|^2)^2/8$ vanishes on the
  3542→$D$-flat direction $H_u = H_d$.  The tree-level lightest
  3543→CP-even Higgs mass is $m_h = m_Z|\cos 2\beta| = 0$---a known
  3544→MSSM result. The meson Yukawa F-terms contribute only mass
  3545→terms ($y_j^2|H|^2$), not quartics.
  3546→In the NMSSM, a singlet coupling
  3547→$\lambda\,S\,H_u\!\cdot\!H_d$ generates an extra quartic
  3548→$\lambda^2|H_u\!\cdot\!H_d|^2$; at $\sin 2\beta = 1$ (i.e.,
  3549→$\tan\beta = 1$), $m_h^2 = \lambda^2 v^2/2$, and
  3550→$\lambda = 0.72$ gives $m_h = 125\GeV$ at tree level.
  3551→The Seiberg Lagrange multiplier~$X$ cannot serve as the NMSSM
  3552→singlet: $[X] = \text{mass}^{-3}$, so the coupling
  3553→$\lambda_0\,X\,H_u\!\cdot\!H_d$ requires
  3554→$[\lambda_0] = \text{mass}^4$; writing
  3555→$\lambda_0 = \hat\lambda\,\Lambda^4$, canonical normalisation
  3556→gives $\lambda_{\text{NMSSM}} = \hat\lambda$.  The Higgs mass
  3557→demands $\hat\lambda \approx 0.51$, but positive meson VEVs
  3558→require $\hat\lambda < 2\Lambda^2/v^2 \approx 3\times 10^{-6}$:
  3559→a five-order-of-magnitude conflict.  Therefore~$S$ must be a
  3560→\emph{separate} elementary singlet with standard NMSSM couplings
  3561→$\lambda_S\,S\,H_u\!\cdot\!H_d + \tfrac{\kappa}{3}\,S^3$.
  3562→At $\tan\beta = 1$ and $\lambda_S = 0.72$, the tree-level
  3563→Higgs mass $m_h = \lambda_S v/\sqrt{2} = 125\GeV$ is recovered.
  3564→The $\mu$-term $\mu = \lambda_S\langle S\rangle$ is set by the
  3565→NMSSM soft potential, leaving $\langle S\rangle$ as a free
  3566→parameter of order the electroweak scale.
  3567→
  3568→\subsection{Vacuum stability, CKM mixing, and the Oakes relation}
  3569→
  3570→For $N_f = N_c = 3$, the Seiberg description uses
  3571→composites $M$, $B$, $\tilde B$ subject to the quantum-modified
  3572→constraint $\det M - B\tilde B = \Lambda^{2N_c}$.  The
  3573→Lagrange multiplier~$X$ enforces this constraint and has
  3574→\emph{no kinetic term}: it is not a dynamical field.
  3575→Three independent arguments establish this:
  3576→(i)~Seiberg's original construction~\cite{Seiberg:1994bz}
  3577→    introduces~$X$ solely to impose the constraint;
  3578→(ii)~the magnetic dual has $N_c' = N_f - N_c = 0$---there is no
  3579→    magnetic gauge group and no ISS-type pseudo-modulus;
  3580→(iii)~the quantum moduli space $\det M - B\tilde B = \Lambda^6$
  3581→    is smooth and requires no additional dynamical field.
  3582→
  3583→Because~$X$ is non-dynamical, $F_X = 0$ at the constrained vacuum:
  3584→the equation $\partial W/\partial X = 0$ is a constraint, not an
  3585→$F$-term energy contribution.  The diagonal seesaw vacuum
  3586→$M^i{}_i = C/m_i$ satisfies the constraint exactly, and all
  3587→off-diagonal meson masses are positive:
  3588→\[
  3589→  m^2(M^a{}_b) = 2\fpi^2 + 2\,|X_0|^2 |M_c|^2
  3590→  \approx 2\fpi^2 = 16{,}928\;\MeV^2\,,
  3591→\]
  3592→where the $F$-term contribution $|X_0|^2|M_c|^2$ is at most
  3593→$5\times 10^{-7}\;\MeV^2$ ($10^{-11}$ of the soft term).
  3594→\emph{There are no tachyonic modes in the off-diagonal meson sector.}
  3595→
  3596→One-loop Coleman--Weinberg corrections are positive
  3597→(stabilising) and suppressed by a factor~$10^{-9}$ relative to
  3598→the tree-level mass.  Moreover, a \emph{fundamental block-diagonal
  3599→obstruction} prevents cross-flavour meson mixing: the $6\times 6$
  3600→off-diagonal mass matrix decomposes into three independent
  3601→$2\times 2$ blocks for conjugate pairs $(M^a{}_b,\,M^b{}_a)$,
  3602→and this structure is preserved by all single-trace polynomial
  3603→K\"ahler invariants.  The physical reason is index contraction:
  3604→at a diagonal vacuum, each $M^a{}_b$ can only contract with its
  3605→conjugate $M^b{}_a$, never with a field from a different
  3606→flavour pair.  Cross-pair mixing would require non-perturbative
  3607→(instanton-generated) K\"ahler corrections or physics beyond the
  3608→confined-meson effective theory.
  3609→
  3610→Although the off-diagonal meson vacuum is exactly diagonal, the
  3611→seesaw structure $M_i = C/m_i$ naturally embeds the
  3612→Gatto--Sartori--Tonin relation.  If a perturbation (from any source)
  3613→were to generate off-diagonal condensates $\epsilon_{ab}$, the
  3614→seesaw hierarchy would ensure
  3615→\[
  3616→  \frac{\epsilon_{us}}{\epsilon_{ud}}
  3617→  = \sqrt{\frac{m_d}{m_s}} = 0.2236
  3618→  = \sin\theta_C\big|_{\text{GST}}\,,
  3619→\]
  3620→reproducing the Weinberg--Oakes relation \emph{algebraically}.
  3621→The GST variant gives $\theta_C = 12.9^\circ$ (PDG:
  3622→$13.04^\circ$, $-0.9\%$).  This is a structural property of the
  3623→seesaw-inverted mass hierarchy, not a dynamical prediction: the
  3624→Cabibbo angle is encoded in $m_d/m_s$ as a free parameter,
  3625→with the seesaw providing the natural scaffolding for the Oakes
  3626→relation.  The actual origin of CKM mixing---whether from
  3627→non-perturbative K\"ahler corrections, physics at the UV
  3628→completion scale, or quark mass-matrix texture---remains an
  3629→open problem.
  3630→
  3631→A notable extension to the first generation:
  3632→the Koide condition does \emph{not} hold for the triple
  3633→$(m_u, m_d, m_s)$ ($Q = 0.567$, 15\% off), but
  3634→$Q(0,\,m_u{+}m_d,\,m_s) = 0.665$ is only $0.27\%$ from~$2/3$---closer
  3635→than the established seed $Q(0,\,m_s,\,m_c) = 0.664$
  3636→at~$0.35\%$.  The Koide chain extends to the first generation but
  3637→sees the isospin-summed mass $m_u + m_d = 6.83\MeV$ as a single
  3638→seed entry; the splitting into $m_u \neq m_d$ is the remaining
  3639→freedom parametrised by~$\theta_C$.
  3640→
  3641→Without a baryon mass term, the global minimum lies at
  3642→$M = 0$, $B\tilde B = -\Lambda^6$, with
  3643→$V = \sum m_i^2 \approx 8{,}750\;\MeV^2$.
  3644→However, the seesaw vacuum is cosmologically stable:
  3645→Coleman--de~Luccia bounce-action estimates give
  3646→$S_4 \sim 2 \times 10^7$ (thick-wall) to $10^9$ (thin-wall),
  3647→both far above the threshold $S_4 > 400$ required for a lifetime
  3648→exceeding the age of the universe.
  3649→Adding a baryon mass term
  3650→$W_B = m_B\,B\tilde B$ to the superpotential lifts the baryonic
  3651→flat direction without affecting the seesaw vacuum
  3652→(since $B = \tilde B = 0$ there): the meson masses $M_i$, the off-diagonal meson spectrum,
  3653→and the Higgs sector are all unchanged.
  3654→With the K\"ahler pole confining $X$ near $X_{\text{seesaw}}$,
  3655→any $m_B \gtrsim 1\;\MeV$ eliminates vacuum~(B) entirely.
  3656→The natural scale $m_B \sim \Lambda = 300\;\MeV$
  3657→(arising from instanton corrections in the confining phase) lifts
  3658→the baryino mass from $|X_0| \sim 10^{-9}\;\MeV$ to $\sim 300\;\MeV$
  3659→and makes $V_B \sim 10^{20}\;\MeV^2 \gg V_A$.
  3660→
  3661→\subsection{The bloom mechanism}
  3662→
  3663→The Koide parametrization
  3664→\begin{equation*}
  3665→  \sqrt{m_k} = v_0\bigl(1 + \sqrt{2}\cos(\delta + 2\pi k/3)\bigr),
  3666→  \quad k = 0,1,2,
  3667→\end{equation*}
  3668→satisfies $Q = 2/3$ identically for all $\delta$ and $v_0$.
  3669→The seed sits at $\delta = 3\pi/4$, where
  3670→$\cos(3\pi/4) = -1/\sqrt{2}$ forces $\sqrt{m_0} = 0$.
  3671→A Koide-preserving bloom is a pure rotation
  3672→$\delta \colon 3\pi/4 \to \delta_{\mathrm{phys}}$ at fixed $v_0$;
  3673→the total mass $M = 6v_0^2$ is conserved while mass redistributes
  3674→among the three eigenvalues.
  3675→
  3676→\paragraph{Adiabatic bion potential.}
  3677→The monopole-instanton effective superpotential on $\mathbb{R}^3\times S^1$
  3678→takes the form $W_{\mathrm{mon}} \sim \zeta(\sqrt{m_1}+\sqrt{m_2}+\sqrt{m_3})$.
  3679→The bion (monopole--anti-monopole) contribution to the
  3680→K\"{a}hler potential is $V_{\mathrm{bion}} = |\!\sum_k s_k\sqrt{m_k}|^2$,
  3681→where $s_k = \mathrm{sign}(z_k)$ are the signs of the
  3682→Koide amplitudes $z_k = v_0(1+\sqrt{2}\cos(\delta+2\pi k/3))$.
  3683→On the interior of the Koide manifold (away from the seed) all
  3684→three $z_k$ are nonzero, and the adiabatic signs follow $\delta$:
  3685→\begin{equation*}
  3686→  \sum_k s_k |z_k| = \sum_k z_k = 3v_0 = \mathrm{const}.
  3687→\end{equation*}
  3688→This is an exact algebraic identity: the adiabatic bion potential
  3689→$V_{\mathrm{bion}} = (3v_0)^2 = 9v_0^2$ is \emph{flat} on the entire Koide
  3690→manifold and cannot select $\delta$.
  3691→
  3692→\paragraph{Frozen-sign bion.}
  3693→At the seed ($\delta = 3\pi/4$), one amplitude vanishes and its sign
  3694→is topologically frozen: for the quark triple $(-s,c,b)$, the
  3695→bloom from seed to physical values takes
  3696→$s_0: 0 \to -1$ while $s_{1,2} = +1$ remain fixed.
  3697→With frozen signs $s_k = +1$ for all $k$, the frozen-sign potential
  3698→\begin{equation*}
  3699→  V_{\mathrm{frozen}}(\delta) = \left(\sum_k |z_k|\right)^2
  3700→\end{equation*}
  3701→is \emph{not} constant on the Koide manifold.  It achieves its
  3702→minimum value $9v_0^2$ at the seed ($\delta = 3\pi/4$, where
  3703→$\sum |z_k| = 3v_0$, the triangle inequality saturated by
  3704→$|z_0| = 0$) and grows for $\delta > 3\pi/4$.
  3705→The frozen bion thus provides a restoring force that opposes the bloom.
  3706→
  3707→\paragraph{Three-instanton driving force.}
  3708→The three-instanton operator
  3709→$\delta W_{3\text{-inst}} = c_3(\det M)^3/\Lambda^{18}$
  3710→arises from the $\mathbb{Z}_{18}$ center symmetry of $SU(3)$ SQCD
  3711→with $N_f = N_c = 3$ (the anomaly coefficient $6N_c = 18$
  3712→breaks $U(1)_{R+2A} \to \mathbb{Z}_{18}$; see the preceding subsection).
  3713→Near the Seiberg vacuum, parametrizing
  3714→$\det M = \Lambda^6 e^{3i\delta}$ gives
  3715→\begin{equation*}
  3716→  V_{3\text{-inst}} \propto -\cos(9\delta).
  3717→\end{equation*}
  3718→For the quark triple $(-s,c,b)$, the observed phase is
  3719→$\delta_{\mathrm{phys}} = 157.2^\circ$.
  3720→The nearest minimum of $-\cos(9\delta)$ is at $\delta = 160^\circ$,
  3721→just $2.8^\circ$ away.
  3722→The combined potential
  3723→\begin{equation*}
  3724→  V(\delta) = A\,V_{\mathrm{frozen}}(\delta) + B\,(-\cos 9\delta)
  3725→\end{equation*}
  3726→has a stable equilibrium at the physical phase for
  3727→$B/A = -2.07$: the three-instanton term drives the bloom outward
  3728→from the seed, while the frozen-sign bion provides the restoring
  3729→force that stabilizes the physical triple.
  3730→
  3731→\paragraph{Lepton triple.}
  3732→For the lepton triple $(e,\mu,\tau)$, all three amplitudes
  3733→$z_k > 0$ throughout the bloom ($\delta_\ell = 132.7^\circ$,
  3734→never crossing zero), so the adiabatic and frozen-sign bion potentials
  3735→coincide: $V_{\mathrm{frozen}} = 9v_0^2$ exactly (flat).
  3736→The bion potential provides no selection.
  3737→The near-rational relation $\delta_\ell \bmod 2\pi/3 \approx 2/9$
  3738→($33\,\mathrm{ppm}$) is therefore \emph{not} explained by the
  3739→bion mechanism; its origin is the three-instanton potential
  3740→$-\cos(9\delta)$ with a minimum at $\delta \equiv 2/9 \pmod{2\pi/3}$,
  3741→as discussed in the preceding subsection.
  3742→The lepton and quark phases are selected by the same instanton
  3743→potential but through different dynamical channels:
  3744→for quarks the frozen-sign bion competes with the instanton term,
  3745→while for leptons the bion is inert and the instanton acts unopposed.
  3746→
  3747→\subsection{The $SO(32)$ projection}
  3748→The adjoint $\mathbf{496}$ contains the sBootstrap
  3749→representations: $\mathbf{24}$ in the neutral sector and
  3750→$\mathbf{15} \oplus \overline{\mathbf{15}}$ in the matter
  3751→sector at $|Q_A| = 2$. The remaining 442 states must be
  3752→projected out or made massive. No projection mechanism has been
  3753→identified. The alternative anomaly-free gauge group
  3754→$E_8 \times E_8$ is blocked by the $\mathbf{15}$ vs.\
  3755→$\mathbf{10}$ obstruction (\S\ref{subsec:so32_open}),
  3756→constraining the string embedding to the Type~I / $SO(32)$
  3757→corner of the landscape.
  3758→
  3759→\subsection{Generation uniqueness}
  3760→The Diophantine system uniquely selects $N = 3$ generations,
  3761→but the third equation ($r^2 + s^2 - 1 = 4N$) has the weakest
  3762→physical motivation. A dynamical derivation of the counting
  3763→constraints would strengthen the argument considerably.
  3764→
  3765→%======================================================================
  3766→\section{Epistemological classification}
  3767→\label{sec:epistemology}
  3768→%======================================================================
  3769→
  3770→The following table classifies the paper's claims by their logical
  3771→status.  ``Derived'' means following from stated assumptions by
  3772→algebra or field theory; ``observed'' means an empirical pattern;
  3773→``assumed'' means an input without derivation.
  3774→
  3775→\begin{center}
  3776→\small
  3777→\begin{tabular}{lll}
  3778→\toprule
  3779→Claim & Status & Comment \\
  3780→\midrule
  3781→$Q = 2/3$ iff $\langle z_k^2\rangle = z_0^2$ & Derived & Algebra (\S\ref{sec:koide_algebra}) \\
  3782→Seed ratio $= 2-\sqrt{3}$ & Derived & From $Q = 2/3$ + one zero \\
  3783→O'R seed at $gv/m = \sqrt{3}$ & Derived & Fermion eigenvalues \\
  3784→K\"ahler stabilization: $c = -1/12$ & Derived & Pole condition (\S\ref{subsec:superpotential}) \\
  3785→$\langle X\rangle/\mu = \sqrt{3}$ in magnetic dual & Derived & ISS dictionary \\
  3786→Pauli theorem (no symmetric $J{=}0$ diquark) & Derived & Angular momentum \\
  3787→Diophantine: $(N,r,s) = (3,3,2)$ unique & Derived & Number theory \\
  3788→Cyclic echo ($t \to c \to s \to \text{stall} \to c$) & Derived & Algebraic necessity \\
  3789→Seed self-dual: $Q(a,b) = Q(1/a,1/b)$ & Derived & Identity for two masses \\
  3790→Cartan angle $\theta = \pi/6 - \delta$ & Derived & Exact trigonometric identity \\
  3791→F-terms flavour-universal: $F = C/v_{\text{EW}}$ & Derived & Seesaw cancellation \\
  3792→\midrule
  3793→Lepton Koide $Q = 2/3$ to $0.001\%$ & Observed & $2.8\sigma$ after LEE \\
  3794→Meson triple $(-\pi,D_s,B)$ to $0.10\%$ & Observed & $1.1\sigma$ after LEE \\
  3795→$v_0$-doubling: $v_0^{(f)}/v_0^{(s)} = 2.0005$ ($0.025\%$); $m_b$ to $0.07\%$ & Observed & Derived via bion K\"ahler \\
  3796→$\delta_\ell \bmod 2\pi/3 = 2/9$ to 33~ppm & Observed & $3.1\sigma$ after LEE \\
  3797→Casimir $R = 0.22310$ vs $\sin^2\theta_W$ & Observed & Scheme-dependent \\
  3798→Dual Koide $Q(1/m_d,1/m_s,1/m_b) = 0.665$ & Observed & $0.2\%$ from $2/3$; not predictive \\
  3799→GST: $|V_{us}| = \sqrt{m_d/m_s} = 0.224$ & Derived & Tachyon ratio; $0.9\%$ from PDG~\cite{GST1968} \\
  3800→$M_0 \approx \fpi^2/27$ & Observed & $0.04\%$; connects lepton scale to $\fpi$ \\
  3801→$\tan\beta = 1$ & Derived & Mixed triples + $SU(5)_f$ \\
  3802→\midrule
  3803→$m_\pi^2 - m_\mu^2 = f_\pi^2$ (VA relation) & Assumed & 2.2\% match \\
  3804→$f_{\text{VA}} = f_\pi$ identification & Assumed & Central ansatz \\
  3805→SU(5) flavor symmetry & Assumed & 5 light quarks \\
  3806→$m_b = 4{,}177 \pm 40\MeV$ from bion minimum & Derived & $0.06\sigma$; requires \"Unsal conjecture \\
  3807→$N_f = 4$ unique in ISS window & Derived & Only integer in $(3, 4.5)$ \\
  3808→ISS vacuum cosmologically stable & Derived & $S_{\text{bounce}} \gg 1$ for $h \sim O(1)$ \\
  3809→ISS CW does not transmit Koide & Derived & $Q_{\text{CW}} \to 1/3$ (affine map) \\
  3810→$c = -1/12$ from bion K\"ahler & Derived & At $\alpha_s = 0.143$ (ISS) \\
  3811→$\text{STr}[M^2] = 18\,\fpi^2$ & Derived & Soft mass only (canonical K\"ahler) \\
  3812→$m_h = \lambda_S v/\sqrt{2} = 125\GeV$ & Input & NMSSM singlet $S \neq X$; $\lambda_S = 0.72$ \\
  3813→$\cos(9\delta)$ from $(\det M)^3/\Lambda^{18}$ & Derived & $\mathbb{Z}_{18}$ charge counting \\
  3814→$\mathcal{Q}_{[ij]} \in \overline{\mathbf{10}}$ of $SU(5)_f$ & Derived & Miyazawa algebra \\
  3815→Casimir structural constraints & Assumed & Reverse-engineered \\
  3816→No tachyons: $F_X = 0$ (Lagrange multiplier) & Derived & Block-diagonal obstruction \\

  3818→$\mu = \lambda_S\langle S\rangle \sim O(v)$ & Free & Standard NMSSM ($S \neq X$) \\

  3821→$X \neq S$: $\hat\lambda_{\text{Higgs}}/\hat\lambda_{\text{max}} \sim 10^5$ & Derived & Dimensional analysis \\

  3832→%======================================================================
  3833→\section{Conclusions}
  3834→\label{sec:conclusions}
  3835→%======================================================================
  3836→
  3837→Seven principal results:
  3838→
  3839→\paragraph{1.\ The Koide formula is energy equipartition.}
  3840→$Q = 2/3$ is $\langle z_k^2\rangle = z_0^2$: perturbation energies 
  3841→match the base scale. The signed-charge framework resolves the 
  3842→electron mass puzzle (near-cancellation, not fine-tuning) and reveals 
  3843→that mesons satisfy Koide with $(-\pi, D_s, B)$ to $0.1\%$.
  3844→
  3845→\paragraph{2.\ Seed triples set the stage for SUSY breaking.}
  3846→The quark seed $(0,s,c)$ and meson seed $(0,\pi,D_s)$ share the
  3847→universal ratio $2 - \sqrt{3}$ and operate at $v_0^2 \sim
  3848→230$--$350\MeV$, close to the lepton triple's $v_0^2 = 314\MeV
  3849→\approx \fpi^2/27$
  3850→(recall that $v_0^2$ carries dimensions of MeV, not MeV$^2$,
  3851→because $v_0 = z_0$ is a charge with dimensions MeV$^{1/2}$). 
  3852→The massless member is the necessary condition for F-term 
  3853→(O'Raifeartaigh) SUSY breaking. Upon breaking, seeds bloom into 
  3854→full triples $(-s,c,b)$ and $(-\pi,D_s,B)$. The lepton triple 
  3855→is the ``almost-seed'' that has barely bloomed.
  3856→
  3857→\paragraph{3.\ The seed condition is one superpotential constraint,
  3858→stabilized by a K\"ahler pole.}
  3859→The O'Raifeartaigh model with superpotential
  3860→$W = f\Phi_0 + m\Phi_1\Phi_2 + g\Phi_0\Phi_1^2$ produces a
  3861→fermionic spectrum $(0,\, m_-,\, m_+)$ that is an exact Koide
  3862→seed triple when $gv/m = \sqrt{3}$. The one-loop CW potential
  3863→alone stabilizes the pseudo-modulus at $v = 0$; a negative
  3864→quartic K\"ahler correction $c\,|\Phi_0|^4/\Lambda_K^2$ with
  3865→$c = -\Lambda_K^2/(12m^2)$ pins it at the K\"ahler pole
  3866→$v = \sqrt{3}\,m/g$ exactly. The Koide seed emerges at
  3867→$M_0 = 2m/3$.  The coefficient $c = -1/12$ is not radiatively
  3868→generated but arises from magnetic bions on
  3869→$\mathbb{R}^3 \times S^1$ at $\alpha_s = 0.143$
  3870→in the ISS magnetic dual.
  3871→
  3872→\paragraph{4.\ Two breaking mechanisms plus a heavy-sector origin.}
  3873→F-term breaking (via the massless seed member) generates the light 
  3874→sector; the Volkov--Akulov relation $f_{\text{VA}} = f_\pi$ is its 
  3875→low-energy realization, not a separate mechanism. D-term breaking 
  3876→($U(1)_{\text{em}}$ FI parameter) provides electromagnetic 
  3877→splittings as a perturbative correction. The heavy triple 
  3878→$(c,b,t)$ stands apart: no seed, no meson shadow, scale far above 
  3879→$\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}$, possibly connected to electroweak breaking.
  3880→
  3881→\paragraph{5.\ The Diophantine counting uniquely selects $N=3$.}
  3882→Three equations on the scalar content---$rs = 2N$, $r(r+1)/2 = 2N$,
  3883→$r^2+s^2-1 = 4N$---have the unique positive-integer solution
  3884→$(N,r,s) = (3,3,2)$, which maps to the $SU(5)$ representations
  3885→$\mathbf{24} \oplus \mathbf{15} \oplus \overline{\mathbf{15}}$.
  3886→This combination is anomaly-free and asymptotically free.
  3887→
  3888→\paragraph{6.\ An algebraic electroweak ratio.}
  3889→The eigenvalue equation $x^2 + s(s+1)\,x - s(s+1) = 0$, with
  3890→$s$ the spin Casimir, predicts
  3891→$R = 1 - x_+(1/2)/x_+(1) = 0.22310$, matching the on-shell
  3892→$\sin^2\theta_W = 0.22320$ to $0.4\sigma$ and giving
  3893→$M_W = 80.374\GeV$ ($0.39\sigma$ from PDG). The equation is
  3894→pinned by three structural constraints; $R$ follows as a
  3895→consequence, not a free parameter. The dynamical origin of the
  3896→equation and the choice of renormalization scheme remain open.
  3897→
  3898→\paragraph{7.\ An explicit Lagrangian with two to three free parameters.}
  3899→The full $\mathcal{N}=1$ Lagrangian
  3900→(\S\ref{subsec:superpotential}) comprises a K\"ahler potential
  3901→with a negative quartic correction $c = -1/12$ (stabilizing
  3902→the pseudo-modulus at $gv/m = \sqrt{3}$) and a bion correction
  3903→$K_{\text{bion}} \sim |\!\sum s_k\sqrt{\hat m_k}|^2$,
  3904→a superpotential combining the Seiberg constraint
  3905→with Yukawa couplings (using separate $H_u$, $H_d$ doublets)
  3906→and an NMSSM coupling $\lambda_S\,S\,H_u\!\cdot\!H_d$
  3907→($\lambda_S = 0.72$ for $m_h = 125\GeV$, with $S$ a separate
  3908→elementary singlet, distinct from the Lagrange multiplier~$X$),
  3909→standard $SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y$ $D$-terms
  3910→(vanishing at $\tan\beta = 1$),
  3911→and soft breaking $V_{\text{soft}} = \fpi^2\,\text{Tr}(M^\dagger M)$.
  3912→The supertrace $\text{STr}[M^2] = 18\,\fpi^2$
  3913→receives contributions only from the nine soft meson masses.
  3914→The mixed up/down-type Koide triples require $\tan\beta = 1$
  3915→for the mass-level condition to equal a Yukawa-level
  3916→condition---a prediction independently motivated by $SU(5)_f$.
  3917→The seed Koide fixes the
  3918→pseudo-modulus VEV $\langle X\rangle = \sqrt{3}\,\mu$ (predicting $m_c/m_s$); the
  3919→bion K\"ahler correction fixes $m_b$ through the
  3920→$v_0$-doubling condition, with Hessian rank~1 constraining
  3921→exactly one mass combination.  The self-consistent chain from $m_s$ alone predicts
  3922→$m_c = 1{,}301\MeV$, $m_b = 4{,}177\MeV$, and---if
  3923→$Q(c,b,t) = 2/3$ is imposed---$m_t = 171.3 \pm 1.5\GeV$
  3924→(Table~\ref{tab:predictions}).
  3925→The lepton Koide scale satisfies $M_0 \approx \fpi^2/27$
  3926→to $0.04\%$, tying lepton masses to the pion decay constant.
  3927→The Seiberg Lagrange multiplier~$X$ has no kinetic term
  3928→($F_X = 0$), and all off-diagonal meson masses are positive;
  3929→a block-diagonal obstruction in the K\"ahler sector prevents
  3930→cross-flavour condensation at any perturbative order.
  3931→The seesaw structure nevertheless embeds the Gatto--Sartori--Tonin
  3932→relation $\sin\theta_C = \sqrt{m_d/m_s}$ algebraically,
  3933→so the Cabibbo angle is naturally encoded in the mass hierarchy
  3934→even though the mixing itself must originate from a mechanism
  3935→beyond the confined-meson effective theory.
  3936→After Koide and $v_0$-doubling constraints,
  3937→only $m_u$, $m_d$, and $m_s$ remain free; $m_u$ and
  3938→$m_d$ are connected to the Cabibbo angle through the
  3939→Gatto--Sartori--Tonin relation.  The $SO(32)$ adjoint provides the UV completion,
  3940→with the $E_8 \times E_8$ alternative blocked by the
  3941→$\mathbf{15}$-vs.-$\mathbf{10}$ obstruction.
  3942→
  3943→The diquark sector, required to complete the SUSY multiplet count,
  3944→fills the symmetric $\mathbf{15} + \overline{\mathbf{15}}$ of
  3945→$SU(5)_f$---the representation produced naturally by the SUSY
  3946→generator acting on quarks and by the $SO(32)$ decomposition. A
  3947→kinematic theorem (\S\ref{subsec:pauli}) shows that \emph{no}
  3948→$J=0$ color-$\bar{3}$ diquark can have symmetric flavor, regardless
  3949→of orbital angular momentum: the sBootstrap diquark in the
  3950→$\mathbf{15}$ cannot be a $qq$ composite. The Miyazawa supercharge
  3951→$\mathcal{Q}_{[ij]} \in \overline{\mathbf{10}}$
  3952→(\S\ref{subsec:baryon_open}) connects mesons to baryons but
  3953→generates diquark operators in $\overline{\mathbf{10}}$
  3954→(antisymmetric), not $\mathbf{15}$ (symmetric).  The remaining
  3955→candidate is a fundamental string-theoretic state from the
  3956→$SO(32)$ adjoint.
  3957→
  3958→The organizing principle is that each sector of the spectrum begins
  3959→with a massless member (a seed), which is the necessary condition
  3960→for spontaneous SUSY breaking. The breaking lifts the zero,
  3961→generates the full triple, and the energy-balance condition
  3962→$\langle z_k^2\rangle = z_0^2$ constrains the initial conditions.
  3963→The bloom from seed to full triple is a phase rotation
  3964→$\delta: 3\pi/4 \to \delta_{\text{phys}}$ in the Koide
  3965→parametrization, at fixed Koide scale $v_0$; it is not a
  3966→continuous deformation of the O'Raifeartaigh spectrum (which would
  3967→break $Q = 2/3$) but a nonperturbative rearrangement.
  3968→The bloom mechanism (preceding section) identifies the
  3969→dynamical origin of $\delta$: the adiabatic bion potential is
  3970→exactly flat on the Koide manifold, but with frozen topological signs
  3971→it provides a restoring force opposing the bloom; the three-instanton
  3972→potential $-\cos(9\delta)$ drives $\delta$ away from the seed, and their
  3973→equilibrium lies within $2.8^\circ$ of the physical quark bloom position.
  3974→The unified superpotential has a layered structure:
  3975→(i)~the Seiberg effective superpotential governs the light sector,
  3976→with SUSY breaking forced by the quantum constraint when $m_u = m_d = 0$;
  3977→(ii)~the O'Raifeartaigh condition $gv/m = \sqrt{3}$ (equivalently
  3978→$\langle X\rangle = \sqrt{3}\,\mu$ in the magnetic dual) fixes the seed Koide ratio,
  3979→predicting $m_c$ from $m_s$;
  3980→(iii)~the bion K\"ahler correction
  3981→$|\!\sum s_k\sqrt{\hat m_k}|^2$ predicts
  3982→$m_b = 4{,}177 \pm 40\MeV$ via $v_0$-doubling;
  3983→(iv)~the Yukawa sector governs the $(c,b,t)$ triple as an
  3984→overdetermination test.
  3985→After these constraints, only two to three free parameters remain:
  3986→$m_u$, $m_d$ (connected to the Cabibbo angle through the GST
  3987→relation), and the overall scale $m_s$. All quark Koide triples
  3988→obligatorily mix up-type and down-type mass eigenstates.
  3989→The energy-balance condition is a boundary condition on the
  3990→superpotential, not an RG attractor; the Koide ratios are naturally
  3991→protected against $D$-term perturbations because $D$-terms shift
  3992→scalar masses but leave fermion masses untouched.
  3993→

  3999→viXra:0809.001 (2008).
